contracts.shape: The sole normative protocol pin is Baton v0.15.0 at upstream commit 16a3b304f360ec9b6a0f2cc5544d019058ac687c, source archive sha256:8acfaaabe27d93cfd6eeb0d8d9fba37261095e9e702826cc9e678f9ab8c3343b, and VERSION blob 86dd09abcea8a8ddd0d020126a0b328c40c5e3c3. Normative vendored JSON bytes are byte-identical to that tag, and the embedded binary plus supported Codex and Claude mirrors report the same pin.
contracts.rationale: Every later record and success path must consume one immutable protocol rather than reinterpret issue history or the running environment.

contracts.shape: spec.references is the only normative discovery surface. Each entry is exactly one contract, sibling-slice, or workspace-root-confined file reference. Resolution rejects duplicate JSON keys, missing or duplicate contract IDs, slice-ID mismatches, non-regular or unreadable files, invalid UTF-8 or JSON, schema-invalid records, release mismatches, traversal, symlink escape, and any unresolved reference before model dispatch. The dedicated spec-ambiguity report remains distinct from generic llm-check-report-v1.
contracts.rationale: Planning and migration are only trustworthy when every normative input is explicit, confined and deterministically resolvable.

contracts.shape: A strict record_version=1 JSON record pins release, protocol name, exact version, upstream SHA, sha256 source digest, vendored VERSION blob OID, origin native or migrated, and authority planning or current. Current operations require one committed marker blob, byte-identical on every participating authority ref and exactly equal to the embedded binary pin. Historical per-record Git evidence permits read-only archive inspection only; missing, dirty-only, shallow, divergent, malformed, unsupported or binary-mismatched evidence fails before side effects.
contracts.rationale: Live mutation authority and truthful historical inspection are separate facts and must both come from committed evidence.

contracts.shape: From immutable start, review and sync commits, the operation emits the exact authored candidate path set, canonical exclusions with reasons, mode-and-blob manifest, normalized prompt diff, provenance tuple, and sha256 input fingerprint. It is NUL-safe and binary-safe, excludes only Baton's named release-record, generated-output and lockfile-only cases, and changes identity whenever any included semantic byte, mode, object identity or accepted provenance changes.
contracts.rationale: Every report, freshness decision and rollback must identify the same semantic candidate without adapter-local path heuristics.

contracts.shape: Each compact status entry pins role, phase, cycle, invocation ID, release-confined full-report path, committed report blob OID, review-scope head, sha256 input fingerprint, PASS or FAIL, and exact blocking finding IDs. The full report and compact entry must agree exactly, duplicate keys and rewritten, missing or deleted blobs fail closed, and only an exact committed report for the unchanged semantic identity may be reused without dispatch.
contracts.rationale: The compact ledger is authority only when it is cryptographically tied to the exact committed full report and semantic input.

contracts.shape: Committed first-parent history enforces Implementer preflight and optional closure, one fresh Verifier authoritative review, cycle 0 then at most one Coach-approved cycle 1 resume, exact dispatch budgets, immutable start and approved implementation heads, append-only report prefixes, and Coach decisions resume_in_scope or re_slice. No adapter, flag or displayed state may waive, reset, reorder or synthesize lifecycle evidence.
contracts.rationale: Role authority is a committed state machine, not a convention reconstructed independently by each public command.

contracts.shape: Only exact paths declared in board.shared_touchpoints may receive contributions from multiple tracks, every contributing track and unique non-empty region must be declared, and the result is Git's canonical committed-blob three-way composition. Merge drivers, attributes, filters, worktree bytes, manual edits and custom conflict resolution cannot influence the result; malformed declarations, non-blob entries, conflicts or tree mismatch block.
contracts.rationale: Parallel tracks are safe only when shared-file composition is declared, deterministic and independent of local Git behavior.

contracts.shape: For each path, active authoritative ownership intervals and rollback-backed retired intervals are derived from committed track ancestry, recognized release-wt synchronization provenance and authoritative review heads. Disjoint sibling evidence remains valid; overlapping later bytes, unrecognized syncs, invalidation tuple mismatch, stale frontiers or historical ownership presented as current PASS evidence block or narrowly invalidate the affected review.
contracts.rationale: Freshness must preserve unrelated verified work while refusing to bless bytes that no current authoritative review covers.

contracts.shape: One predicate validates exact protocol authority, schema and record identity, lifecycle terminal state, report blob identity, semantic scope, evidence freshness, shared composition, rollback equality and deferral legitimacy. A valid unstarted deferral and a rollback-backed terminal re-slice deferral are distinct accepted cases; raw deferred state is never sufficient. Every normal and idempotent merge, release and shipping path reruns the predicate.
contracts.rationale: All adapters need one conservative answer to whether committed evidence authorizes integration.

contracts.shape: sworn maintainability review accepts only Implementer preflight or closure and Verifier authoritative role-phase pairs; sworn maintainability adjudicate accepts only resume_in_scope or re_slice. Both derive lifecycle inputs from committed owner-track state, require a clean start, atomically write coherent report, compact status and journal transitions, commit and push the exact track ref before protocol success, durably persist valid FAIL, and recover a commit-without-push by reusing the exact report with zero model dispatch. No dry-run, no-push, force, waiver or lifecycle override exists.
contracts.rationale: The public operation that owns a lifecycle transition must also own its durable restart boundary.

contracts.shape: sworn ship is a read-only pre-cutover readiness gate and never records deployment. sworn mark-shipped is the post-deployment operation that revalidates the same committed protocol, lifecycle, report, provenance, rollback and integration-ready evidence before atomically moving eligible verified slices to shipped. Neither command deploys code, and idempotent reruns revalidate all gates rather than trusting displayed state.
contracts.rationale: Readiness to deploy and truthful post-deployment state are distinct auditable facts.

contracts.shape: A human-ratified Planner delta fixes source ref and object identities, exact owner track and status blob, intended record mutations, rollback or replacement linkage, target propagation refs and recovery state. The engine validates then atomically commits and propagates only that delta, preserving maintainability byte-for-byte except an explicitly ratified rollback_slice_id. In-place protocol migration is legal only for pristine planned records with null start, empty actual files and reports, null adjudication and pending verification; the receipt records before and after blob identities and never fabricates PASS evidence.
contracts.rationale: Planner judgment remains human-led while record mutation and propagation become deterministic and restart-safe.
